name: Core Data Models
description: Pydantic v2 frozen models for Event (content-addressable SHA-256 ID from canonical JSON of identity fields), Story (open→closed lifecycle with ordered events, close reasons, chain_by support), CorrelationRule (group_by/chain_by/terminal_event/timeout), and all configuration models (source configs, sink configs, memory limits, kindex noteworthiness filter). Also defines SourceProtocol and SinkProtocol as runtime_checkable Protocol classes. This is the shared data model used by every other Chronicler component. Covers AC1 (Event model), AC2 (Story model), AC16 (config models, protocols).

types.name: EventKind

fields.name: value
fields.description: The event kind string

validators.error_message: EventKind must be 1-255 chars, lowercase alphanumeric with dots and underscores, starting with a letter
EventKind must be between 1 and 255 characters

types.description: A dot-separated event kind identifier (e.g. 'ci.build.completed', 'incident.opened'). Must be non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric with dots and underscores.

types.name: EntityKind

fields.name: value
fields.description: The entity kind string

validators.error_message: EntityKind must be 1-255 chars, lowercase alphanumeric with dots and underscores, starting with a letter
EntityKind must be between 1 and 255 characters

types.description: The kind/type of the entity this event pertains to (e.g. 'repository', 'pipeline', 'incident'). Lowercase alphanumeric with dots and underscores.

types.name: EntityId

fields.name: value
fields.description: The entity identifier string

validators.error_message: EntityId must be between 1 and 1024 characters

types.description: A non-empty identifier for the entity this event pertains to. Free-form but non-empty, max 1024 chars.

types.name: ActorId

fields.name: value
fields.description: The actor identifier string

validators.error_message: ActorId must be between 1 and 512 characters

types.description: Identifier for the actor that caused the event (user, service, system). Non-empty, max 512 chars.

types.name: Sha256Hex

fields.name: value
fields.description: The hex-encoded SHA-256 hash

validators.error_message: Must be exactly 64 lowercase hex characters (SHA-256)

types.description: A lowercase hex-encoded SHA-256 hash (64 characters).

types.name: StoryId

fields.name: value
fields.description: UUID4 string

validators.error_message: StoryId must be a valid UUID4 in lowercase canonical form

types.description: UUID4 string identifier for a Story.

types.name: AwareDatetime

fields.name: value
fields.description: ISO 8601 datetime with timezone (e.g. '2024-01-15T10:30:00Z')

validators.error_message: Timestamp must be timezone-aware (not naive)

types.description: A timezone-aware ISO 8601 datetime string. Naive datetimes are rejected at validation time. Internally stored as Python datetime with tzinfo set.

types.name: ContextValue
types.description: Allowed value types within an Event context dict. Constrained to types that produce deterministic canonical JSON.

types.name: ContextDict

fields.name: value
fields.description: Key-value context data

validators.expression: all(isinstance(k, str) and len(k) > 0 for k in value.keys())
validators.error_message: All context keys must be non-empty strings

validators.expression: all(isinstance(v, (str, int, float, bool, type(None))) for v in value.values())
validators.error_message: Context values must be str, int, float, bool, or None

types.description: A dict[str, ContextValue] — the flexible context bag on an Event. Keys are non-empty strings. Values are constrained to str|int|float|bool|None for deterministic canonical JSON hashing.

types.name: CorrelationKeys

fields.name: value
fields.description: Correlation key-value pairs

validators.expression: all(isinstance(k, str) and len(k) > 0 and isinstance(v, str) and len(v) > 0 for k, v in value.items())
validators.error_message: All correlation keys and values must be non-empty strings

types.description: A dict[str, str] of correlation key-value pairs used for grouping events into stories. All keys and values must be non-empty strings.

types.name: StoryStatus
types.description: Lifecycle status of a Story.

types.name: CloseReason
types.description: Reason why a Story was closed.

types.name: Event

fields.name: id
fields.description: Content-addressable SHA-256 hex digest of canonical JSON of identity fields. Computed automatically by model_validator(mode='before'); must not be set manually unless replaying.

fields.name: event_kind
fields.description: Dot-separated kind of event (e.g. 'ci.build.completed').

fields.name: entity_kind
fields.description: Kind of entity this event pertains to (e.g. 'repository').

fields.name: entity_id
fields.description: Identifier for the specific entity instance.

fields.name: actor
fields.description: Who or what caused this event.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: When the event occurred. Must be timezone-aware.

fields.name: context
fields.description: Flexible key-value context data. Values constrained to str|int|float|bool|None for deterministic hashing.

fields.name: correlation_keys
fields.description: Key-value pairs used by CorrelationRules to group events into stories.

fields.name: source
fields.description: Provenance label indicating which source produced this event. Excluded from content hash.

types.description: Immutable event envelope (frozen=True). The id field is a content-addressable SHA-256 hex digest computed via model_validator(mode='before') from canonical JSON of the identity fields: event_kind, entity_kind, entity_id, actor, timestamp, context, correlation_keys. Canonical JSON uses json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=True, default=str) on a fixed-key dict of those fields, with timestamps serialized as ISO 8601 with 'Z' suffix. The 'source' field is excluded from the hash (provenance metadata only).

types.name: Story

fields.name: story_id
fields.description: UUID4 identifier for this story.

fields.name: story_type
fields.description: Type label for this story, derived from the CorrelationRule.story_type.

validators.error_message: story_type must be between 1 and 255 characters

fields.name: correlation_rule
fields.description: Name of the CorrelationRule that created this story.

validators.error_message: correlation_rule name must be between 1 and 255 characters

fields.name: group_key
fields.description: The group_by key-value pairs that define this story's identity within its rule.

fields.name: events
fields.description: Ordered tuple of event IDs (SHA-256 hex) belonging to this story.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Lifecycle status: 'open' or 'closed'.

fields.name: start_ts
fields.description: Timestamp of the first event added to this story.

fields.name: end_ts
fields.description: Timestamp when the story was closed. Required when status is 'closed', must be absent/None when 'open'.

fields.name: duration
fields.description: Duration in seconds from start_ts to end_ts. Set when closed.

validators.error_message: Duration must be non-negative

fields.name: event_count
fields.description: Number of events in this story. Must equal len(events).

validators.error_message: event_count must be non-negative

fields.name: close_reason
fields.description: Reason for closing: 'terminal' (terminal event matched), 'timeout' (rule timeout elapsed), 'evicted' (memory pressure). Required when closed, must be absent/None when open.

fields.name: parent_story_id
fields.description: If this story was created via chain_by from a closed story, the parent story's ID.

types.description: Immutable story model (frozen=True). Represents a correlated sequence of events with an open→closed lifecycle. Mutation is performed via model_copy(update={...}) to produce new Story instances. A model_validator(mode='after') enforces that closed stories have end_ts and close_reason set, and open stories have neither. Events are stored as a tuple of event ID strings (not full Event objects) for frozen compatibility and copy efficiency.

types.name: EventIdTuple
types.description: An ordered tuple of event IDs (SHA-256 hex strings). Stored as tuple for frozen model hashability.

types.name: TerminalEventPattern

fields.name: value
fields.description: Field path to expected value mappings

validators.expression: all(isinstance(k, str) and len(k) > 0 and isinstance(v, str) for k, v in value.items())
validators.error_message: Terminal event pattern keys must be non-empty strings and values must be strings

types.description: A dict[str, str] representing a simple field=value pattern match on an event. All specified fields must match for the event to be considered terminal. Keys are dot-path field references (e.g. 'event_kind', 'context.status').

types.name: GroupByKeys
types.description: Ordered tuple of field paths used for grouping events into stories (e.g. ('correlation_keys.repo', 'correlation_keys.branch')). Order matters for deterministic group key construction.

types.name: ChainByKeys
types.description: Ordered tuple of field paths linking a closed story to a new story via chain_by semantics.

types.name: CorrelationRule

fields.name: name
fields.description: Unique name for this correlation rule.

validators.error_message: Rule name must be 1-255 chars, alphanumeric with underscores/dots/hyphens, starting with a letter

fields.name: event_kinds
fields.description: Set of event kinds this rule applies to. An event must have an event_kind in this set to be considered by this rule.

fields.name: group_by
fields.description: Ordered tuple of field paths used to compute the group key. Events with the same group key values are correlated into the same story.

fields.name: chain_by
fields.description: Optional ordered tuple of field paths for chain_by semantics. When a story is closed by terminal event, a new story is opened if a subsequent event matches these fields from the closed story.

fields.name: terminal_event
fields.description: Optional simple field=value pattern. When an event matches all specified fields, the story is closed with close_reason='terminal'.

fields.name: timeout
fields.description: Optional timeout in seconds. If no new event arrives within this duration after the last event, the story is closed with close_reason='timeout'.

validators.error_message: Timeout must be positive (at least 1ms)

fields.name: story_type
fields.description: The story_type label assigned to stories created by this rule.

validators.error_message: story_type must be between 1 and 255 characters

types.description: Immutable rule definition (frozen=True) for correlating events into stories. Defines which event kinds to match, how to group them, optional chaining from closed stories, terminal event detection, and timeout.

types.name: EventKindSet

fields.name: value
fields.description: Set of event kind strings (stored as frozenset for hashability)

validators.error_message: event_kinds must contain at least one event kind

types.description: A frozenset of EventKind strings. Used for unordered set membership checks in CorrelationRule.event_kinds.

types.name: SourceType
types.description: Discriminator for source configuration types.

types.name: WebhookSourceConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'webhook'.

fields.name: host
fields.description: Bind address for the webhook HTTP server.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port to listen on.

validators.error_message: Port must be between 1 and 65535

fields.name: path
fields.description: URL path to accept POSTs on.

validators.error_message: Path must start with / and contain only alphanumeric, underscore, hyphen, slash

fields.name: source_label
fields.description: Value to set in Event.source for events from this source.

types.description: Configuration for the HTTP POST webhook source.

types.name: OtlpSourceConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'otlp'.

fields.name: host
fields.description: Bind address for the OTLP gRPC server.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port to listen on.

validators.error_message: Port must be between 1 and 65535

fields.name: source_label
fields.description: Value to set in Event.source for events from this source.

types.description: Configuration for the OTLP gRPC collector source.

types.name: FileSourceConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'file'.

fields.name: path
fields.description: Path to the JSONL file to tail.

validators.error_message: File path must be between 1 and 4096 characters

fields.name: poll_interval_seconds
fields.description: How often to poll for new lines in seconds.

validators.error_message: Poll interval must be between 0.1 and 3600 seconds

fields.name: source_label
fields.description: Value to set in Event.source for events from this source.

types.description: Configuration for the JSONL file tailer source.

types.name: SentinelSourceConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'sentinel'.

fields.name: host
fields.description: Bind address.

fields.name: port
fields.description: Port to listen on.

validators.error_message: Port must be between 1 and 65535

fields.name: source_label
fields.description: Value to set in Event.source for events from this source.

types.description: Configuration for the Sentinel incident receiver source.

types.name: SourceConfig
types.description: Discriminated union of all source configuration types, discriminated by the 'type' field.

types.name: SinkType
types.description: Discriminator for sink configuration types.

types.name: StigmergySinkConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'stigmergy'.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Stigmergy endpoint URL.

validators.error_message: URL must start with http:// or https://

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

validators.error_message: Timeout must be between 0.5 and 300 seconds

types.description: Configuration for emitting events/stories to Stigmergy.

types.name: ApprenticeSinkConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'apprentice'.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Apprentice endpoint URL.

validators.error_message: URL must start with http:// or https://

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

validators.error_message: Timeout must be between 0.5 and 300 seconds

types.description: Configuration for emitting events/stories to Apprentice.

types.name: DiskSinkConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'disk'.

fields.name: events_path
fields.description: Path for the events JSONL file.

validators.error_message: Path must be between 1 and 4096 characters

fields.name: stories_path
fields.description: Path for the stories JSONL file.

validators.error_message: Path must be between 1 and 4096 characters

types.description: Configuration for the JSONL disk persistence sink.

types.name: NoteworthinessFilter

fields.name: min_event_count
fields.description: Minimum number of events in a story for it to be noteworthy.

validators.error_message: min_event_count must be at least 1

fields.name: min_duration_seconds
fields.description: Minimum story duration in seconds for noteworthiness.

validators.error_message: min_duration_seconds must be non-negative

fields.name: story_types
fields.description: If non-empty, only stories with these story_types are noteworthy. Empty means all types qualify.

fields.name: exclude_close_reasons
fields.description: Close reasons to exclude from noteworthiness (e.g. ['evicted'] to skip evicted stories).

types.description: Configuration for filtering which stories are considered noteworthy for Kindex knowledge capture.

types.name: KindexSinkConfig

fields.name: type
fields.description: Discriminator, always 'kindex'.

fields.name: url
fields.description: Kindex endpoint URL.

validators.error_message: URL must start with http:// or https://

fields.name: timeout_seconds
fields.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds.

validators.error_message: Timeout must be between 0.5 and 300 seconds

fields.name: noteworthiness
fields.description: Filter controlling which stories are forwarded to Kindex.

types.description: Configuration for the Kindex knowledge capture sink.

types.name: SinkConfig
types.description: Discriminated union of all sink configuration types, discriminated by the 'type' field.

types.name: MemoryLimitsConfig

fields.name: max_open_stories
fields.description: Maximum number of concurrently open stories. When exceeded, oldest stories are evicted with close_reason='evicted'.

validators.error_message: max_open_stories must be between 1 and 1,000,000

fields.name: max_events_per_story
fields.description: Maximum number of events in a single story. When exceeded, the story is closed with close_reason='evicted'.

validators.error_message: max_events_per_story must be between 1 and 100,000

types.description: Configuration for memory pressure management in the story manager.

types.name: ChroniclerConfig

fields.name: sources
fields.description: List of source configurations (at least one required).

validators.error_message: At least one source must be configured

fields.name: sinks
fields.description: List of sink configurations (at least one required).

validators.error_message: At least one sink must be configured

fields.name: correlation_rules
fields.description: List of CorrelationRule definitions (at least one required).

validators.error_message: At least one correlation rule must be configured

fields.name: memory_limits
fields.description: Memory pressure management configuration.

fields.name: state_path
fields.description: Directory path for JSONL state persistence files.

validators.error_message: state_path must be between 1 and 4096 characters

types.description: Top-level Chronicler configuration loaded from YAML. Validated at startup; invalid config is a fatal error.

types.name: EventOrStory
types.description: Union type for SinkProtocol.emit — a sink may receive either an Event or a Story.

functions.name: compute_event_id
functions.description: Computes the content-addressable SHA-256 hex ID for an Event from its identity fields. Constructs a fixed-key dict of {event_kind, entity_kind, entity_id, actor, timestamp, context, correlation_keys}, serializes to canonical JSON (json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=True, default=str)) with timestamps as ISO 8601 'Z' suffix, then returns the lowercase hex SHA-256 digest. This is a pure function with no side effects.

error_cases.name: naive_timestamp
error_cases.condition: timestamp has no tzinfo (naive datetime)

error_data.message: Event timestamp must be timezone-aware

error_cases.name: invalid_context_value_type
error_cases.condition: A value in the context dict is not str|int|float|bool|None

error_data.message: Context values must be str, int, float, bool, or None

preconditions: timestamp must be timezone-aware (has tzinfo)
All context values must be str|int|float|bool|None
All correlation_keys values must be non-empty strings
postconditions: Returned string is exactly 64 lowercase hex characters
Same inputs always produce the same output (deterministic)
Different inputs with any single field changed produce a different output (collision-resistant)

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of identity fields

functions.name: create_event
functions.description: Creates a new Event instance. The id field is automatically computed via compute_event_id from the identity fields using the model_validator(mode='before'). This is the primary factory for Event creation. Returns a frozen Pydantic model instance.

error_cases.name: invalid_event_kind
error_cases.condition: event_kind does not match the EventKind pattern

error_cases.name: invalid_entity_kind
error_cases.condition: entity_kind does not match the EntityKind pattern

error_cases.name: empty_entity_id
error_cases.condition: entity_id is empty

error_cases.name: empty_actor
error_cases.condition: actor is empty

error_cases.name: naive_timestamp
error_cases.condition: timestamp has no tzinfo

error_cases.name: invalid_context_value
error_cases.condition: A context value is not str|int|float|bool|None

error_cases.name: invalid_correlation_key
error_cases.condition: A correlation key or value is empty or not a string

preconditions: event_kind matches ^[a-z][a-z0-9_.]{0,254}$
entity_kind matches ^[a-z][a-z0-9_.]{0,254}$
entity_id is non-empty, max 1024 chars
actor is non-empty, max 512 chars
timestamp is timezone-aware
All context values are str|int|float|bool|None
All correlation_keys keys and values are non-empty strings
postconditions: Event.id is the SHA-256 of canonical JSON of identity fields
Creating the same event twice produces Events with identical id fields
Event.source is excluded from the hash computation

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is total size of all fields

functions.name: create_story
functions.description: Creates a new open Story instance with a generated UUID4 story_id, the given story_type and correlation_rule name, an initial group_key, and the first event ID. Status is set to 'open', end_ts/duration/close_reason are None.

inputs.name: story_type
inputs.description: Story type label from the CorrelationRule.

inputs.name: group_key
inputs.description: Group key values for this story.

inputs.name: first_event_id
inputs.description: ID of the first event in this story.

inputs.name: parent_story_id
inputs.description: Optional parent story ID for chain_by linkage.

error_cases.name: empty_story_type
error_cases.condition: story_type is empty

error_cases.name: empty_correlation_rule
error_cases.condition: correlation_rule is empty

error_cases.name: invalid_first_event_id
error_cases.condition: first_event_id is not a valid SHA-256 hex string

preconditions: story_type is non-empty
correlation_rule is non-empty
first_event_id is a valid SHA-256 hex string
start_ts is timezone-aware

functions.name: append_event_to_story
functions.description: Produces a new Story with an additional event appended. Uses model_copy(update={...}) on the source story to add the event ID to events, increment event_count. The story must be open.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The existing open story to append to.

error_cases.name: story_not_open
error_cases.condition: story.status != 'open'

error_data.message: Cannot append event to a closed story

error_cases.name: duplicate_event_id
error_cases.condition: event_id already in story.events

error_data.message: Event ID already exists in story

preconditions: story.status == 'open'
event_id is a valid SHA-256 hex string
event_id is not already in story.events (no duplicates)

functions.name: close_story
functions.description: Produces a new Story with status='closed', the given close_reason, end_ts set, and duration computed as (end_ts - start_ts).total_seconds(). Uses model_copy(update={...}) on the source story.

inputs.name: story
inputs.description: The existing open story to close.

inputs.name: close_reason
inputs.description: Why the story is being closed: 'terminal', 'timeout', or 'evicted'.

inputs.name: end_ts
inputs.description: The timestamp at which the story is closed.

error_cases.name: story_already_closed
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'closed'

error_data.message: Cannot close an already closed story

error_cases.name: end_before_start
error_cases.condition: end_ts < story.start_ts

error_data.message: end_ts must not be before start_ts

preconditions: story.status == 'open'
end_ts >= story.start_ts
end_ts is timezone-aware

functions.name: validate_story_consistency
functions.description: Validates the internal consistency of a Story model. Checks that: open stories have no end_ts/duration/close_reason; closed stories have all three set; event_count matches len(events); duration matches (end_ts - start_ts).total_seconds() for closed stories. This is used by the model_validator(mode='after') and can also be called explicitly for testing.

error_cases.name: open_story_has_end_ts
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'open' and story.end_ts is not None

error_data.message: Open story must not have end_ts

error_cases.name: open_story_has_close_reason
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'open' and story.close_reason is not None

error_data.message: Open story must not have close_reason

error_cases.name: open_story_has_duration
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'open' and story.duration is not None

error_data.message: Open story must not have duration

error_cases.name: closed_story_missing_end_ts
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'closed' and story.end_ts is None

error_data.message: Closed story must have end_ts

error_cases.name: closed_story_missing_close_reason
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'closed' and story.close_reason is None

error_data.message: Closed story must have close_reason

error_cases.name: closed_story_missing_duration
error_cases.condition: story.status == 'closed' and story.duration is None

error_data.message: Closed story must have duration

error_cases.name: event_count_mismatch
error_cases.condition: story.event_count != len(story.events)

error_data.message: event_count must equal len(events)

postconditions: Returns True if story is internally consistent
Raises ValidationError if any consistency check fails

functions.name: matches_terminal_pattern
functions.description: Checks whether an Event matches a TerminalEventPattern from a CorrelationRule. For each key-value pair in the pattern, the event must have a matching value at the specified field path. Supports 'event_kind', 'entity_kind', 'entity_id', 'actor' as direct field paths, and 'context.<key>' or 'correlation_keys.<key>' for nested access.

inputs.name: pattern
inputs.description: The terminal event pattern (field path→expected value pairs).

error_cases.name: invalid_field_path
error_cases.condition: A pattern key is not a recognized field path (not event_kind/entity_kind/entity_id/actor/context.*/correlation_keys.*)

error_data.message: Unrecognized terminal event pattern field path

preconditions: event is a valid Event instance
pattern keys are valid field paths
postconditions: Returns True if all pattern entries match the event's field values
Returns False if any pattern entry does not match or field does not exist
Empty pattern matches all events (vacuously true)

performance_budget.complexity: O(p) where p is number of pattern entries

functions.name: extract_group_key
functions.description: Extracts the group key values from an Event according to a CorrelationRule's group_by field paths. Returns a CorrelationKeys dict mapping each group_by path to the corresponding value from the event. Field paths reference 'correlation_keys.<key>', 'context.<key>', or top-level event fields.

inputs.name: event
inputs.description: The event to extract group key from.

inputs.name: group_by
inputs.description: Ordered tuple of field paths to extract.

error_cases.name: missing_field
error_cases.condition: A group_by field path does not resolve to a value on the event

error_data.message: Event is missing required group_by field

error_cases.name: non_string_value
error_cases.condition: A resolved field value is not a string and cannot be deterministically converted

error_data.message: Group key field must resolve to a string value

preconditions: event is a valid Event instance
group_by contains at least one field path
All field paths in group_by resolve to string values on the event
postconditions: Returned dict has exactly len(group_by) entries
Each key in the returned dict is the field path from group_by
Each value is the string representation of the event's field at that path

performance_budget.complexity: O(g) where g is number of group_by fields

functions.name: load_chronicler_config
functions.description: Loads and validates a ChroniclerConfig from a YAML file path. Uses Pydantic v2 validation on the parsed YAML. Fails fast with a clear error if the config is invalid (fatal startup error per SOP). Validates all source/sink configs via discriminated unions, all CorrelationRules, and memory limits.

inputs.name: config_path
inputs.description: Filesystem path to the YAML configuration file.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: config_path does not exist or is not readable

error_data.message: Configuration file not found

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: File content is not valid YAML

error_data.message: Configuration file contains invalid YAML

error_cases.name: validation_error
error_cases.condition: Parsed YAML does not conform to ChroniclerConfig schema

error_data.message: Configuration validation failed

error_cases.name: duplicate_rule_names
error_cases.condition: Two or more CorrelationRules have the same name

error_data.message: CorrelationRule names must be unique

error_cases.name: empty_sources
error_cases.condition: No sources configured

error_data.message: At least one source must be configured

error_cases.name: empty_sinks
error_cases.condition: No sinks configured

error_data.message: At least one sink must be configured

error_cases.name: empty_rules
error_cases.condition: No correlation rules configured

error_data.message: At least one correlation rule must be configured

preconditions: config_path is a readable file path
File contains valid YAML
postconditions: All source configs are valid instances of their discriminated types
All sink configs are valid instances of their discriminated types
memory_limits has valid max_open_stories and max_events_per_story
At least one source, one sink, and one correlation rule are configured

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the YAML configuration file from disk

functions.name: event_to_dict
functions.description: Serializes an Event to a plain dict suitable for JSON serialization. Uses Pydantic's model_dump(mode='json'). Timestamps are serialized as ISO 8601 strings. The id field is included.
preconditions: event is a valid Event instance
postconditions: Returned dict contains all Event fields
Round-tripping through event_from_dict(event_to_dict(e)) produces an Event with the same id

functions.name: event_from_dict
functions.description: Deserializes an Event from a plain dict (e.g. parsed from JSON). Validates all fields. If 'id' is present in the dict, it is verified against the computed hash; if it mismatches, a ValidationError is raised. If 'id' is absent, it is computed.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The dict to deserialize into an Event.

error_cases.name: missing_required_field
error_cases.condition: A required field (event_kind, entity_kind, entity_id, actor, timestamp, context, correlation_keys) is missing

error_data.message: Missing required event field

error_cases.name: id_mismatch
error_cases.condition: The provided 'id' field does not match the computed SHA-256 hash

error_data.message: Event ID does not match computed content hash

error_cases.name: invalid_field_value
error_cases.condition: A field value fails validation (e.g. naive timestamp, invalid event_kind pattern)

error_data.message: Event field validation failed

preconditions: data contains at minimum: event_kind, entity_kind, entity_id, actor, timestamp, context, correlation_keys
postconditions: Returned Event is frozen and valid
Event.id matches the content hash of identity fields
If input dict had an 'id' field, it matches the computed id

functions.name: story_to_dict
functions.description: Serializes a Story to a plain dict suitable for JSON serialization. Uses Pydantic's model_dump(mode='json'). Events tuple is serialized as a JSON array of ID strings.
preconditions: story is a valid Story instance
postconditions: Returned dict contains all Story fields
events field is a list of SHA-256 hex strings
Timestamp fields are ISO 8601 strings or null

functions.name: story_from_dict
functions.description: Deserializes a Story from a plain dict (e.g. parsed from JSON). Validates all fields including the lifecycle consistency checks (model_validator(mode='after')).

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The dict to deserialize into a Story.

error_cases.name: missing_required_field
error_cases.condition: A required field is missing from the dict

error_data.message: Missing required story field

error_cases.name: lifecycle_inconsistency
error_cases.condition: Story lifecycle invariants are violated (e.g. open story with end_ts)

error_data.message: Story lifecycle consistency check failed

error_cases.name: event_count_mismatch
error_cases.condition: event_count does not match len(events)

error_data.message: event_count must equal len(events)

preconditions: data contains all required Story fields
postconditions: Returned Story is frozen and valid

invariants: All Event instances are frozen (immutable) — no attribute can be reassigned after construction
All Story instances are frozen (immutable) — mutation only via model_copy(update={...}) producing a new instance
All CorrelationRule instances are frozen (immutable)
Event.id is always the SHA-256 hex digest of canonical JSON of identity fields (event_kind, entity_kind, entity_id, actor, timestamp, context, correlation_keys)
Canonical JSON uses json.dumps(sort_keys=True, separators=(',',':'), ensure_ascii=True, default=str) with timestamps as ISO 8601 'Z' suffix
Two Events with identical identity fields always have the same id (content-addressable)
Event.source is excluded from the content hash — it is provenance metadata only
An open Story has status='open', end_ts=None, duration=None, close_reason=None
A closed Story has status='closed', end_ts set (timezone-aware), duration set (>=0), close_reason set to 'terminal'|'timeout'|'evicted'
Story.event_count always equals len(Story.events)
Story.events is a tuple of SHA-256 hex event ID strings (ordered by append time)
Story.duration (when set) equals (end_ts - start_ts).total_seconds()
CorrelationRule.event_kinds is a frozenset with at least one member
CorrelationRule.group_by is a non-empty tuple
All timestamps in Event and Story are timezone-aware (AwareDatetime) — naive datetimes are rejected at validation
SourceProtocol is a runtime_checkable typing.Protocol with async start(), async stop(), and subscribe(callback) methods
SinkProtocol is a runtime_checkable typing.Protocol with async start(), async stop(), and async emit(item: Event|Story) methods
ChroniclerConfig requires at least one source, one sink, and one correlation rule
CorrelationRule names are unique within a ChroniclerConfig
All config models use Pydantic v2 with frozen=True

data_access.rationale: Pure data model definitions with no I/O. Event ID computation performs in-memory SHA-256 hashing of canonical JSON. Story and CorrelationRule models are immutable value objects. No external data is read or written; all data resides in-process as Pydantic model instances.

authority.rationale: This component is the single authoritative definition for all Chronicler data models: Event, Story, CorrelationRule, configuration structures, and the SourceProtocol/SinkProtocol interfaces. All other components depend on these canonical types.
